page: The Vultr vhf-4c-16gb is a virtual machine instance in the High Frequency Compute family. It features an x86_64 architecture powered by an Intel Core Processor (Skylake, IBRS) running at 2.0 GHz. The configuration includes 4 vCPUs mapped to 4 physical cores with a shared CPU allocation. It is equipped with 16.0 GB of system memory, providing 4.0 GB per core. Local storage is bundled with a 384 GB NVMe SSD, and the instance includes one complimentary public IPv4 address. No GPU accelerators are present.

Benchmark results indicate a mix of performance characteristics. The instance demonstrates strong single-core CPU performance in stress-ng tests, placing in the top 25%. However, multi-core CPU performance and PassMark CPU scores fall into the average range. Memory bandwidth tests show average performance for small, cached blocks (497.33 GB/sec) but drop to weak levels for large, uncached read and write blocks. Cryptographic and compression workloads, such as PassMark encryption and gzip compression, also register in the bottom 25%. For database operations, Redis SET operations achieve an average of 6.0 million operations per second, while PassMark database operations are weak.

The shared CPU allocation presents a potential tradeoff in environments requiring sustained, predictable multi-core performance, as reflected in the average multi-core and weak physics benchmark results. From a qualitative cost-efficiency perspective, the inclusion of a 384 GB NVMe SSD provides a high storage-to-compute ratio for budget-conscious deployments. This server is suited for general-purpose workloads, basic web serving, lightweight Redis caching, and single-threaded applications that benefit from its strong single-core performance.
description: The Vultr vhf-4c-16gb is an x86_64 virtual server within the High Frequency Compute family, configured with 4 shared Intel Skylake vCPUs running at 2.0 GHz, 16.0 GB of RAM, and a bundled 384 GB NVMe SSD. In benchmark evaluations, the instance exhibits strong single-core CPU performance, ranking in the top 25% for stress-ng single-core tests. Conversely, multi-core CPU performance, PassMark CPU scores, and cached memory bandwidth for small blocks fall into the average range. Large-block memory bandwidth, gzip compression, and database operations show weaker performance profiles. Given its shared CPU allocation and solid local NVMe storage capacity, this instance offers a cost-effective option for workloads that do not require sustained multi-core execution. It is best suited for general-purpose applications, single-threaded tasks, lightweight Redis caching, and basic web serving.
og_description: Vultr vhf-4c-16gb is a High Frequency Compute instance featuring 4 shared Intel vCPUs, 16 GB RAM, and a 384 GB NVMe SSD, delivering strong single-core performance alongside average multi-core capabilities.
meta_description: Vultr vhf-4c-16gb is an x86_64 server with 4 shared Intel vCPUs, 16 GB RAM, and a 384 GB NVMe SSD, suited for general-purpose workloads.
tagline: A shared-core virtual server combining strong single-core processing with generous local NVMe storage for balanced, budget-friendly workloads.
bullet_points: 4 shared Intel Skylake vCPUs and 16.0 GB of system memory
Bundled 384 GB NVMe SSD local storage
Strong single-core CPU performance in stress-ng benchmarks
Average multi-core CPU and Redis SET performance
Suited for general-purpose applications and single-threaded tasks
